item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The link in the article is broke, find actual like to the picture HERE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just got around to the double Declassified post on reddit for this and 1800. I think this was the post I was missing to really make GAW stand out as a 'real' GoI in my mind. I really appreciate bluntfiend as a character thanks to this post.

My biggest issue is, obviously, how scattered the tone is. The flow of the story whiplashes from YouTube poop humor to Jude's recounting of his in-universe experience with 1800. Incredibly jarring, and made this piece a lot harder for me to enjoy.

That said, I enjoyed it thoroughly by the end, as a standalone article, as a 'sequel' to 1800, and as a meta commentary of the community's growth. If we had just one of these clever, self-aware posts for every 5000 internet comments criticizing the wiki for the old-hat issues, the community would be so much better off.

But, since a lot of people don't like voting based on what an article 'represents,' I'll say +1 solely citing the quality of writing and execution as justification.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kinda surprised nobody pointed this out yet:

$\frac{(3m)^3}{173mL/day} \approx 420 \text{ years}$

https://www.wolframalpha.com/input/?i=%283m%29%5E3%2F%28%28176mL%29%2Fday%29+in+years

well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: its kinda criminal that this has less of a score than 1800

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it.

item.title: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TQdn-O5MDYA
Re: Downvoting hard
item.content:encoded:
IDK how or where you decided that "dignity" was a word that applied to this vvebsite but I can tell you right now that your illusions would have been shattered sooner or later. This entire thing is a silly, fairly inconsequential project that's not terribly plausible even if you accept the premise of magic being real.

That's not to say that you can't take it seriously or expect a certain standard of realism, it's just that none of this was never "dignified" to participate in even BEFORE we decided to start referencing stupid memes, and that's not a meaningful metric to judge it on.

item.title: MOD POST - INFO - OPEN
item.content:encoded:
You're straying dangerously close to breaking Wheaton's law here.

Wish I could downvote twice.

And the constant use of pejoratives such as "stupid" are not really what I'd call 'constructive' criticism.

Please be more judicious in the future.

item.title: Downvoting hard
item.content:encoded:
1800 is legitimately one of my favorite SCP's and AWCY? is my favorite GoI.

This article is a perfect demonstration of how wrong AWCY? has gone in recent years. It went from an extremely mysterious art terrorist organization more concerned with promoting their "art" than any law or sense of morality to a collection of meme spouting hipsters making practical jokes.

Honestly, even the concept of a AWCY? member developing a conscience about the group's actions can work (see SCP-3337) but a stupid youtube meme reference as their grand critique?
It just makes me lose respect for everyone involved. Jude looks uninspired and stupid. AWCY? looks incompetent for allowing this to be made. The Foundation looks silly while trying to describe it seriously. The concept of Anomalous Objects in general loses dignity because here's a physics defying otherworldly object that does nothing but reference a stupid meme.

Wish I could downvote twice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a really late response. A year late. But I hope to elucidate why Jude acted the way he did.

It all has to do with the line: "Interviewed: Jude Kriyot. Male. 23 years old."
It all has to do with the line: "Interviewed: Jude Kriyot. Male. 23 years old."

So, there's a link to "Different Kinds of Nihilism" in the text. Specifically, in Jude's breakdown.

It's really easy to miss, but "Different Kinds of Nihilism" specifically mentions two things: "It had to have been 2008." and "You were only twenty. You were a fool. A stupid fool."

Then, it fast forwards after a chat log…

"It's 2012. It could be any other year. It doesn't matter. They all feel the same. An unending stream. You've tried to kill yourself a few times by then."

The date during which Jude is 23 therefore must be either 2011 or 2012. It shows Jude during this period as a terribly broken man, doing everything he can to stop thinking to the best of his ability. I don't doubt the original intention was that he's like this due to killing a room of people. But I can't imagine that his complacence regarding 1800 isn't weighing hard on his mind.

Not to mention, I imagine this is one of the only times he's been even able to talk truly and frankly about what happened to another human being.

As for how he specifically reacted to the question… It's one thing to try and deal with one's personal trauma in a personal way, and another entirely to have to confront that when it's brought up against your plans. It intruded on Jude. What's more, his reaction isn't abrupt. That is to say, Jude tries to avoid it entirely at first. He's working himself up to try and continue, and he wants to get past it, and he's terse at first. He keeps getting pressed, and then… he slips.

He figures that it's all he's going to get when he's talking about it. He's hesitant, he can't think straight. He wants to get it all out but he keeps slipping and getting more and more upset.

That latter part is likely what you were looking for, but I figured the easter egg was relevant enough to talk about. Anyway, I hope this explains why he acted why he did.

item.title: Tentatively Upvoting
item.content:encoded:
I like it in-universe as a way of lashing out against SCP-1800, don't like that it's seemingly intended to do so in a non-diegetic way as well. SCP-1800 is one of my favorite SCP's in that it's the moral event horizon for AWCY; at the risk of sounding like a huge weeb, it's their "Berserk eclipse" moment. AWCY will do anything for their art- up to and including the worst shit imaginable. I feel like saying "Ugh SCP-1800 is bad and should never have been written because it makes people do bad shit" is a bit like saying "Ugh that Jason character is bad and never should have been written because he murders people"
Still, it works in the context of the universe and like it for that reason. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm upvoting but I just want to say that I love this comment because it would work equally well in-universe, just replace "article" with "piece".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah I can see where you're coming from.

But personally I found that the article relied too much on critiquing 1800 and barely stands on its own as a result.

If you like it, fine, let's just agree to disagree.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mean, there's a difference between writing a response to a thing and bashing it, isn't it? This isn't bashing it. I think bashing it would be if you wrote an SCP about how it destroyed all shitty art pieces, including 1800 (which incidentally, I have read as a draft, and it was a really good draft). This is more thoughtful and responding to the article and critiquing it via art.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh I didn't find this particularly funny and it relies way too much on bashing 1800. (And this is coming from someone who doesn't like that article either.)

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The few things I loved about that Skooks video, snow outside the ice and Fred and Shaggy smoking blunts, are not there. It's understandable though due to being a character piece and a crtique on AWCY but this still doesn't endear me to GAW.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, it's good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Could you note that Jude Kriyot's PoI number is PoI-6870?

Edit: Misercordia I don't think you were supposed to do that. It might get reverted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whoops, didn't think the first could be taken like that. Wording changed to make it more clear. As for the second, while I personally think it wouldn't kill them, I know trauma fucks with some people's morality. Additionally, there might've not even been enough time that's passed for the foundation to find out. Don't see much reason to clarify for those reasons :P

Anyway, thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Two quick criticisms. Regarding the criteria for Alpha-1168, zero is a number. Additionally, it's not clear if SCP-1168-1 survives or not… being frozen like that would usually be lethal, but Jude is usually pretty anti-killing-people and plenty of media has people survive that, and it's an anomaly so if he wanted it to not kill them it won't.

+1 anyway though, I love this thing overall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"When SCP-1168-1 and SCP-1168-2 simultaneously exist, SCP-1168 will deactivate, and SCP-1168-1 will instantly materialize a wooden club and be encased in a 3m x 3m x 3m cube of ice."

I literally had to look away for like ten seconds. You're a bad person.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first I thought this was too silly, but then I realized I was being a huge square by thinking that.

THEN I looked even deeper and realize that this was a true jab at AWCY's Minotaur and their other pieces, and I loved it more. Taking those guys down a notch, gotta appreciate that. AWCY sometimes comes off as a group of elitist assholes who's art is more important than life and being decent to people, yaknow? if all this stuff existed I'd probably hold a little resentment to AWCY for their blatant disregard for innocent civilian life for the sake of "art," which is why I appreciate Gamers Against Weed so much.

In the end the creepy and bizzare are more my cup of tea, but every once in a while a silly one can make me smile. Plus skooks is probably one of my favorite YT series. You get + rating from me sir~

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Im my opinion, I find the interview to be a bit too gratuitous (mainly the long outburst). Perhaps it might work better as an audio element than written text, but this does not leave a satisfying end to an otherwise serviceable parody.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As I have previously asserted, I am not completely in the GAW loop. However, as someone who knows trauma and has failed to write it well, I can say that this succeeds where I failed. Purely from that perspective, this is a good work of fiction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should I read 1800 before I read this? Because I'm interested in both.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When I first saw GAW introduced, the last thing I expected was that it would be all about character. This continues a welcome trend in GAW works, and I don't mind the anti-1800 nature of the skip, as the impetus is essentially the same in-universe as on the wiki.

The only thing I don't get: if Jude did create this statue, then why does he break down and describe the events relating to 1800? It makes sense for him to break down if the question ("did you see the statue move?") surprises him and reminds him unexpectedly of a past trauma. But if he made this statue, he has been thinking about that trauma, and would be thinking about it still while the Foundation speaks to him - it doesn't ring true that he would deliver the backstory quite so emotionally.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm far too turned off by the metafictional element here. I mean, making Youtube Poops in real life has a certain charm to it, but "take that"s are just not worth it, as I have found out apparently.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Last line of the SCP reads "current evidence suggests Mr. Kriyot was the creator of the object." Jude lied about creating it to get the Foundation off his back.

However, other than the implication he was blackmailed into activating it, Jude's being genuine.

The main impetus for him creating the statue, then, and why it mirrors 1800 so closely, is because it was designed to get catharsis from having "someone he knew" be a victim to it.

As well, the mirroring is a catharsis due to having been a part of AWCY to begin with, to satirize how inane The Minotaur was while claiming it art. Specifically, the Chuck-E-Cheese tokens takes a potshot at The Minotaur taking coins, the middle finger point to Minotaur's point, the "ritualized interactions" being nothing but a meme, etc.

As for the character, he's further characterized in both the crosslinks to Different Kinds of Nihilism and The Assassination of Beleaguered Normalcy by the Coward Gamers Against Weed, as well as different GAW tales, if you'd like to further understand him. I'd recommend it; it might give some further depth to this, and they're all wonderful reads.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm a little confused. At first I thought this was just a tweaking of an earlier SCP, but this statue targets people different from 1800? Sex offenders instead of innocents?

And the interviewee was somehow connected to 1800, it killed someone he knew, and he…I don't know, was blackmailed into activating 1168 for some reason?

There's a story here, but I can't make the connection.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a well done mix of humor and character development, and is much better than the article being parodied.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a very good combo of "fuck you" and character study.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like how Jude is written in the addendum, I think it works off of what 1800 has in place and, from what I understand about the rest of GAW, why it became a splinter cell. +1 and I'm excited to see more of this GOI!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
good article detected, upvote administered.

btw a link in the interview to the-critic is borked.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I need to upvote this at least 300 times.

Edit: no but seriously, if this gets more votes than 1800 I will be very happy. :p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I read this before SCP-1800 and I didn't really like it at first. Then, after I read SCP-1800, I read back through it and loved it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good that it's more than just a critique of an article that hasn't aged well; it's also great at character. Better than the draft I read.

item.title: No Disrespect Intended For AWCY Or Any Of Their Fine Classic SCPs
item.content:encoded:
Thank you to everyone who critted this work, including but not limited to:

LilyFlower
Rimple
WrongJohnSilver
Decibelle
Scantron/Communism will win
Kinchtheknifeblade

And anyone who my shitty memory forewent.

For context as to its creation: I dislike SCP-1800. That's pretty much the impetus.

edit: SHIT. credit to sandrewswann for sections carried over from 1800's text, which was heavily consulted in 1168's creation. I apologize for the tardiness on this; I thought the mere mention of 1800 would've been sufficient credit, as it was a textual source, but I'm going to make this more explicit because it needs to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This just really, really feels like something Jude would make. It's really good.

Also on another note, this scp is a good look into the community's new nature. Which is to say not writing weird rape articles anymore.
